title: eMuleBB Rust product-family campaign
description: Campaign for the eMuleBB Rust 0.0.3 headless core client. It reuses the shared eMuleBB campaign runner and local deterministic swarm policy for REST, ED2K, Kad, transfer, upload, queue, and local indexing proof, with public live-wire proof deferred until a hide.me-bound Rust live scenario exists.

scenarios.title: Rust client repo materializes from the eMuleBB org

evidence.description: Workspace deps.json contains emulebb-rust resolving to the emulebb organization repository.
Focused pytest coverage builds emulebb-rust through Cargo, launches local goed2k-server, and writes configured LAN REST and P2P binds from X_LOCAL_IP into the generated Rust config.

scenarios.title: Rust local eD2K search and download proof

evidence.description: The focused Rust pytest scenario proves configured P2P bind, local goed2k server search, concurrent Rust peer transfers including Unicode filenames, hash-only ED2K metadata recovery, source fallback, download, pause persistence, destructive transfer file deletion, and core client status visibility.

The Rust protocol-combination matrix reuses the stock eMuleBB local cases for plain server traffic, obfuscated-preferred server traffic, obfuscated-required server traffic, and obfuscated-required transfer with server UDP disabled while downloading Unicode-named fixtures from the tracing harness through the shared goed2k launcher. The suite records a protocol_matrix_coverage check requiring all four cases plus plain, obfuscated, UDP-disabled, compressible-fixture, low-compressibility fixture, Unicode fixture-name, and hash-only fixture-name surfaces, and each case requires ED2K link name round-trip, three Rust transfers in one protocol session, hash-only ED2K link metadata recovery in every protocol case, completed large-file bytes, source userHash metadata, and MD4/AICH hashset metadata for both named transfers.

scenarios.title: Rust and eMuleBB bidirectional local swarm proof

evidence.description: The existing multi-client P2P matrix requires the Rust/eMuleBB bidirectional scenario, which delegates to the shared cross-client suite and goed2k launcher, starts emulebb-rust and eMuleBB with configured LAN or VPN binds, proves Rust-to-eMuleBB transfer with a Unicode filename, then proves eMuleBB-to-Rust transfer with a Unicode filename through canonical REST APIs with Rust-persisted source userHash, MD4 hashset, and AICH hashset metadata.

scenarios.title: Rust and aMule bidirectional compatibility proof

evidence.description: The existing multi-client P2P matrix requires the Rust/aMule bidirectional scenario, which reuses the staged aMule daemon/control adapter, shared goed2k launcher, configured LAN control bind, short-path aMule profile roots, and Rust REST control to prove aMule downloads from Rust and Rust downloads from aMule with Rust-persisted source userHash and MD4 hashset metadata while recording aMule's missing AICH hashset.

Focused pytest coverage first compares the emulebb-rust route table to the eMuleBB-owned /api/v1 contract, then exercises the Rust server, Kad, search, transfer create, transfer control, shared-file, upload/queue, diagnostic, and transfer file deletion envelopes without private aliases or shims.

scenarios.title: Rust hide.me-bound live-wire proof
localInputs: operator-owned live-wire inputs stay in ignored local files
public live Rust P2P must bind through hide.me or an explicit VPN bind from configuration

evidence.description: Add first-class Rust live-wire automation only after the Rust runtime supports the same configured VPN binding contract used by eMuleBB live campaigns.
Use existing aMuTorrent controller scenarios once Rust implements the required eMuleBB REST endpoints without private adapters, aliases, or compatibility shims.

scenarios.title: Rust local indexing and resource stress

evidence.description: Reuse eMuleBB resource evidence structure for SQLite index, FTS, buffered I/O, RAM, handles, transfer runtime, upload queue, and REST counters as 0.0.x storage scales beyond the local proof.

Define Rust package artifacts and SBOM rows under the same naming structure as eMuleBB: campaign id emulebb-rust, future annotated release tag emulebb-rust-v0.0.3 after release proof and operator tagging approval, release root emulebb-rust-v0.0.3, and standard ZIP assets named emulebb-rust-0.0.3-ARCH.zip.
